title: The Arm's Length Principle
text: Cornerstone of OECD TP. Treats associated enterprises as if they were independent entities. Comparability analysis: 5 factors — contractual terms, functions/assets/risks (DEMPE for IP), characteristics of property/service, economic circumstances, business strategies.

title: Transfer Pricing Methods
text: Five methods recognized: CUP (Comparable Uncontrolled Price), Resale Price Method, Cost Plus Method, TNMM (Transactional Net Margin Method), Profit Split Method. Selection per the 'most appropriate method' criterion.

title: Special considerations for intangibles + DEMPE
text: DEMPE = Development, Enhancement, Maintenance, Protection, Exploitation. Returns to intangibles must align with where DEMPE functions are performed + risks assumed + assets used. Hard-to-Value Intangibles (HTVI) require ex-post adjustment if actual outcomes diverge significantly from projections.

title: Financial Transactions
text: Intercompany loans, guarantees, cash pools, hedging, captive insurance, captive financing. Loan: must reflect lender + borrower perspective + arm's-length interest. Cash pool: short-term, balanced participation, arm's-length yield. Captive insurance: real risk transfer + risk distribution + diversification or no recognition.

title: Three-tier TP documentation
text: Master File (group-level — org chart, IP, financing, group-wide TP policies), Local File (entity-level transactions + methodology + comparables), Country-by-Country Report (CbCR for MNE groups > €750M consolidated revenue — revenue / income / taxes / employees / assets by jurisdiction).

title: Aligning TP outcomes with value creation
text: Action 8 (intangibles): returns follow DEMPE + control of risk. Action 9 (risk + capital): risk allocations must align with control + financial capacity. Action 10 (high-risk transactions): low value-adding intra-group services 5% markup safe harbor; profit-split applicability.

title: Pillar Two Global Minimum Tax
text: 15% minimum effective tax rate for MNE groups with consolidated revenue > €750M. Mechanisms: Income Inclusion Rule (IIR — top-up tax at parent), Undertaxed Profits Rule (UTPR — at intermediate / sister entities), Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (QDMTT — at source). Substance-based income exclusion (SBIE): 5% × payroll + 5% × tangible assets (with phase-in from 10% / 8% over 10 years).

title: Treas. Reg. 1.482-1(c) Best Method
text: US TP requires the BEST METHOD — the method that, under the facts + circumstances, provides the most reliable measure of an arm's-length result. Comparability + quality of data are the primary considerations. No strict hierarchy of methods.

title: Treas. Reg. 1.482-9 Services
text: US service-cost method (SCM): controlled services transaction may be evaluated using total services costs with no markup IF the services qualify as low-margin covered services + meet the business judgment rule. Specified services list at 1.482-9(b)(4) + business judgment exclusion at 1.482-9(b)(5).

title: Limited-Risk Distributor (LRD)
text: LRD = local distributor with limited inventory + market + currency + customer-credit risk. Typically remunerated on routine basis (operating margin in 1-4% range, depending on industry + functions). LRDs should NOT generate losses absent extraordinary circumstances; sustained LRD losses are a red flag for TP audit.

title: Hard-to-Value Intangibles
text: HTVI = intangibles where (i) no reliable comparables exist + (ii) projections used in valuation are highly uncertain at transfer time. Tax authorities may use ex-post evidence to evaluate ex-ante arm's-length pricing. Exceptions: detailed contemporaneous projections, unforeseeable changes, audit completion.

title: Cost Contribution Arrangements
text: CCA = participants share costs + risks of developing / producing / obtaining assets, services, or rights, with expected benefits. Participants must have reasonable expectations of benefit. Contributions must be valued at arm's-length. Balancing payments required when contributions vs benefits diverge.
